narrative:
Skies are partly sunny with unseasonably chilly temperatures and a brisk northwesterly wind. It’s the last day of our cool May set before we flip the switch into a more summertime weather regime. Friday will have a big surge in temperatures, and that only continues over the weekend as above-normal readings will return. Rainfall chances will be rather pitiful, with only afternoon shower or thundershower strays by Sunday and into the early part of next week.

Uncharacteristically Brisk

Last Updated Thu May 14 at 10:30AM by Scott Stephens

Skies are partly sunny with unseasonably chilly temperatures and a brisk northwesterly wind. It’s the last day of our cool May set before we flip the switch into a more summertime weather regime. Friday will have a big surge in temperatures, and that only continues over the weekend as above-normal readings will return. Rainfall chances will be rather pitiful, with only afternoon shower or thundershower strays by Sunday and into the early part of next week.

Forecast Discussion

Mountain weather is under the influence of an upper low across the Mid-Atlantic states today, and that will promote cool and breezy conditions. If the wind can die-down enough tonight, there could be a few more valley frost patches by daybreak Friday.

After that, it’s all about the warming trend.

Friday will start out sunny but there will be some extra afternoon clouds to accompany the surge in temperatures. Similarly, Saturday will feature a good deal of cloudiness, but even warmer conditions.

Sunday highs will be nearly 10-degrees above normal, with just the slightest risk for an afternoon shower or thundershower.

More of the same weather can be expected for Monday and Tuesday, as we receive our summer preview.

A cold front inches closer by midweek, with a bump-up in afternoon thundershower potential. The potential for the front to either get stuck or move very slowly may add to our rainfall prospects just after the end of this forecast cycle.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
